methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Synhelion is a Series C climate and energy company that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private comparables—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—alongside one public reference point and eight additional private peers in the Climate & Energy sector.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $80M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $136M. The Peer-Median method references the median valuation across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, which stands at $1.0B (range: $700M–$2.0B). This wide range reflects material variance in how investors price frontier-tech energy companies at Series C, even within a narrow cohort.

The triangulated analysis produces a low estimate of $116M, a median of $568M, and a high of $1.1B. The median sits substantially above the stage-progression result but materially below the peer median, reflecting tension between Synhelion's fundraising to date and peer valuations in this segment. Key uncertainties include technology readiness relative to peer maturity, capital intensity of path to commercialization, and whether the peer median reflects comparable risk profiles or sector-wide froth. A valuation range of $300M–$700M may be most defensible given the data constraints.
